nodes.name: Get Project Config1
nodes.notes: Fetch AISMR project configuration from database

nodes.name: Generate Ideas
nodes.notes: Calls idea-generator.fn with DB persona prompt

json.userInput: Let's create an aismr video about different baby birds. We should see hand interacting with the chicks

json.prompt:
1) STYLE / GENRE — Surreal ASMR micro‑film; cinematic, tactile, serene, physically impossible yet shot with real-world optics.

2) SCENE DESCRIPTION — A baby chick perches quietly on a thick, cushiony surface of impossibly deep velvet, each feather seamlessly blending into the surrounding fabric. The chick’s down and the velvet beneath it are one continuous, matte-black expanse—every subtle movement causes the velvet and feathers to ripple together, swallowing light and glowing faintly along the curves. Surrounding dust motes and micro-fibers float, caught in soft volumetric haze. No on-screen text anywhere.

3) CINEMATOGRAPHY — 9:16 vertical (1080×1920); 75mm lens, ultra-shallow DOF, 180° shutter. Slow dolly-in with gentle leftward drift for deeply cinematic parallax; velvet’s matte surface absorbs light, while subtle rim-lighting outlines undulating contours. Inertia of lens movement reads as physically real.

4) ACTIONS & TIMING —
0.0–3.0s: Camera glides in to establish chick and velvet unity; surface undulates with the chick’s slow breathing.
3.0–7.0s: The chick shifts, and velvet ripples radiate outward, swallowing and then revealing faint highlights; occasional feather-fibers twinkle and dissolve back into the fabric. 5.0s: Dry whisper: "Velvet Chick."
7.0–10.0s: Ultra-macro on the velvet surface as a single feather merges into the matte depths, then fade to black as dust motes shimmer in silhouette.

5) AUDIO / ASMR — Ambient vacuum-bed at ~–28 dBFS; hyper-detailed foley: soft fiber brushes, muffled ruffling, faint plush compressions. Whisper “Velvet Chick” at 5.0s (dry, centered, no reverb). No other dialogue.

6) MUSIC / SCORE — Gentle, ethereal ambient. 0–3s: calm, low pads rise softly; 3–7s: warm, pillowy chords swell; 7–10s: drift into airy, serene decay. All music ~–12 LUFS.

7) COLOR / GRADE — Palette: deep matte black, muted midnight blue, soft ash gray, faint lavender, velvety ivory. Tone is calming, intimate, and minimal—soft cool highlights and immersive shadow detail.

8) NOTES / CONSTRAINTS — 10 seconds, one continuous vertical shot (9:16); no scene cuts. Velvet/feather mechanics and their unity must be visible. Mandatory whisper ("Velvet Chick") at 5.0s, dry. Fade to black with shimmering fiber particles. No on-screen text or extra dialogue.

9) SINGLE-LINE SUMMARY — A 10-second surreal ASMR micro-film for October: Velvet Chick — a serene baby bird and its velvet world merge as one, pulsing with intimate, impossible calm.

1) STYLE / GENRE — Surreal ASMR micro‑film; cinematic, tactile, awe-striking yet gentle, physically plausible lighting.

2) SCENE DESCRIPTION — On a shadowed, velvety pedestal sits a tiny baby bird entirely shaped from luminous, prismatic glass. Its delicate form refracts beams of soft morning light into spectral color patches on nearby surfaces. With every subtle motion—a flutter, a blink—fine cracks shimmer and flow smoothly across its body, impossibly repairing themselves. Interior bubbles and imperfections create shifting rainbows as internal reflections move. The setting is close, dreamy, dappled with floating dust, no on‑screen text.

3) CINEMATOGRAPHY — 9:16 vertical (1080×1920); 80mm lens, shallow DOF focused tight on the chick’s glass head and chest. 180° shutter for natural inertia. Slow dolly‑in and gentle right orbit produce shifting reflections and lens flares, with macro dust drifting in foreground parallax.

4) ACTIONS & TIMING —
0.0–3.0s: Camera glides inward, catching rainbows as the glass chick exhales; minute internal bubbles and light scatters drift through its body.
3.0–7.0s: The chick turns and twitches its wings; cracks and swirls pulse within, refracting the light, brilliant spectral glows on chest and eyes. At 5.0s, chick tilts beak at the lens.
5.0s: Single, dry whisper: "Glass Chick."
7.0–10.0s: Macro focus on one prismatic crack sealing itself; rays of color flicker, dust motes shimmer, and shot fades to black with lingering light particles.

5) AUDIO / ASMR — Ambient vacuum-bed at ~–28 dBFS; hyper-real foley: fine tings, faint creaks, glass friction, subtle breath. 5.0s: Whisper “Glass Chick” (dry, center, minimal reverb), surrounded by micro shatters and gentle tick sounds.

6) MUSIC / SCORE — Ethereal, airy pads and fragile high notes. 0–3s: gentle crystalline rise; 3–7s: iridescent, swelling chords; 7–10s: music dissolves to thin, shimmering tail, holding ~–12 LUFS.

7) COLOR / GRADE — Palette: frosted clear, prism white, warm gold, indigo blue, blush pink. Emphasizes "awe, fragile wonder" with diffused light, soft-focus contrast, radiant spectral highlights.

8) NOTES / CONSTRAINTS — 10 seconds, single vertical 9:16 shot; no scene cuts; mandatory dry whisper at 5.0s: “Glass Chick.” Fade to black with signature prismatic particle shimmer. Impossible self-repair and refracting optics must be shown.

9) SINGLE-LINE SUMMARY — A 10-second surreal ASMR micro-film for October: Glass Chick — a prismatic, glass-bodied baby bird pulses with living light and impossible self-repair, shimmering with awe and fragile wonder.

1) STYLE / GENRE — Surreal ASMR micro‑film; cinematic, tactile, impossible yet filmable, sensorial first.

2) SCENE DESCRIPTION — Extreme macro: a baby chick is sculpted entirely from dense, crystalline frost. Its texture bristles with tiny ice feathers that crackle and subtly shift, light refracting through facets as cold vapor gently swirls around it. Each motion—blink, breath, micro-fluff—sends fine frost crystals showering and reforming, impossible to maintain outside the impossible chill. The environment is an intimate, midnight-cold surface dusted with snow and shimmering with particles, suspended in absolute stillness. No on-screen text.

3) CINEMATOGRAPHY — 9:16 vertical (1080×1920); 80mm macro lens; shallow DOF isolates chick's frost-detailed face, icy plumage and rising vapor. 180° shutter. Slow dolly-in with subtle rightward orbit, creating refractive parallax with ice particles and haze. Inertia and lens weight preserved.

4) ACTIONS & TIMING —
0.0–3.0s: Camera glides in, crisp frost crackles and breath vapor rises. Macro focus highlights spiked ice feathers, frost crystals drift in cold air.
3.0–7.0s: Chick shifts, icy feathers bristle and snap. Breath freezes instantly, micro shatters sparkle. 5.0s: Whisper begins: "Frost Chick."
7.0–10.0s: Ultra-close-up: fractal frost grows and erases itself over the eye; cold dust motes shimmer as stillness deepens into fade out.

5) AUDIO / ASMR — Sub-bass frozen ambience at ~–28 dBFS, layered with icy crackle, brittle feather foley, inhale/exhale vapor hiss, granular crystallizing sounds. Dry, center whisper at 5.0s: "Frost Chick."

6) MUSIC / SCORE — Ethereal, glacial ambient; resonant high pads and buried icy arpeggios. 0–3s: icy suspense builds; 3–7s: distant, tense swells and static; 7–10s: permafrost tail, music fades into crystalline echoes. ~–12 LUFS.

7) COLOR / GRADE — Palette: glacial blue, pure white, argent silver, midnight navy, violet ice. Scene glows with frozen, shadowed lowlights, crisp highlights, and iridescent shimmer, echoing "tense icy stillness."

8) NOTES / CONSTRAINTS — 10.0 seconds, single vertical (9:16) continuous shot, 80mm macro, shallow DOF, 180° shutter. No scene cuts. Dry whisper at 5.0s: "Frost Chick." Fade to black with signature icy particle shimmer. Frost/ice mechanics are always visible. No on-screen text.

9) SINGLE-LINE SUMMARY — A 10-second surreal ASMR micro-film for October: Frost Chick — a baby bird made of crackling frost pulses and shivers with tense, icy stillness in macro cinematic detail.

1) STYLE / GENRE — Surreal ASMR micro‑film; tactile, cinematic, dreamlike, impossible-but-filmed.

2) SCENE DESCRIPTION — In macro scale, a baby chick sculpted from glinting, liquid mercury stands atop a matte, dark stone inside a softly luminous vignette. The chick’s metallic surface flows with smooth, playful undulations—droplets detach, hover, and rejoin as if defying gravity. Its form flexes, feathers morphing as mirrored ripples sweep under the skin. Mercury’s surface tension creates bulbous, lively refractions that catch camera and set light. Suspended fine dust and faint mist envelop the space. No on-screen text.

3) CINEMATOGRAPHY — Vertical 9:16 (1080×1920), 80mm macro lens, shallow DOF tightly frames the chick’s head and body. 180° shutter applies physical camera inertia. Slow dolly-in with rightward orbit creates dynamic metallic reflections and liquid parallax.

4) ACTIONS & TIMING —
0.0–3.0s: Camera glides inward, revealing the mercury chick and its swirling, mirrored flow. Light sweeps across undulating feathers.
3.0–7.0s: Chick twitches, droplets split off and orbit playfully, then reabsorb. Surface flexes with radiant ripples; internal reflections shimmer.
5.0s: Dry, center whisper: “Mercury Chick.”
7.0–10.0s: Ultra-macro close-up: a mercury bead hovers, then merges in a perfect, silent splash. Scene fades to black with silver particle shimmer.

5) AUDIO / ASMR — Ambient bed ~–28 dBFS: metallic undercurrent, liquid flicks, soft popping beads, rapid micro-drips. Whisper at 5.0s—"Mercury Chick," dry and centered, no effect.

6) MUSIC / SCORE — Ethereal, fluid pads with brief metallic harmonics: 0–3s playful rise; 3–7s swelling shimmer; 7–10s light, rippling tail with silvery high end; ~–12 LUFS.

7) COLOR / GRADE — Palette: chrome silver, gunmetal gray, blue steel, black velvet, white highlight. Lighting is cool yet lively, reflecting a "playful liquid metal" mood—color pops in lens flares and ripple refraction.

8) NOTES / CONSTRAINTS — 10 seconds, single continuous vertical (9:16) shot; macro lens, shallow DOF; no scene cuts or text; whisper "Mercury Chick" at 5.0s mandatory; fade out with liquid-metal shimmer. Impossible effect: mercury moves and morphs naturally, never cartooned.

9) SINGLE-LINE SUMMARY — A 10-second surreal ASMR micro-film for October: Mercury Chick — a baby bird formed of playful liquid metal ripples, flexes, and shimmers in impossible macro detail.

1) STYLE / GENRE — Surreal ASMR micro‑film; nostalgic, tactile, dreamlike, physically impossible but convincingly filmed.

2) SCENE DESCRIPTION — In extreme macro, a tiny baby chick is completely composed of lush, living moss, delicate shoots and miniature leaves forming soft contours for beak, wings, and eyes. The chick gently stands atop a moist stone surrounded by blurred, dew-flecked forest floor micro-landscape. Water droplets bead and roll on the moss down, and with each slight movement, the moss appears to slow-breathe: tiny fronds pulse, sprouting and retracting, impossible in real-time. A nostalgic haze fills the air, with golden particulates drifting through dappled sunlight. No on‑screen text.

3) CINEMATOGRAPHY — 9:16 vertical (1080×1920), 78mm macro lens, shallow DOF to isolate moss chick’s head and chest; 180° shutter. Slow dolly-in with gentle rightward orbit reveals mossy textures, parallax from foreground dew and glimmering specks; all camera inertia and lens falloff physically real.

4) ACTIONS & TIMING —
0.0–3.0s: Camera glides in on chick, dew beads glint, moss "breathes" gently, soft sunlight halos it.
3.0–7.0s: Chick blinks, moss fronds unfold and retract in gentle waves, dropping miniature spores and droplets that fall upward, defying gravity.
5.0s: Whisper: “Moss Chick.”
7.0–10.0s: Macro close-up on a moss tendril slowly coiling around a dew drop; golden dust swirls as shot fades to black and faint particle shimmer.

5) AUDIO / ASMR — Whisper-quiet ambient bed at –28 dBFS; layers: soft leaf bristle, faint dew drips, moist earth movement, spore puff sounds. At 5.0s, dry center whisper: “Moss Chick.”

6) MUSIC / SCORE — Ethereal, gentle ambient: 0–3s soft nostalgia-tinged pads rise; 3–7s mellow warm swells; 7–10s subtle dissolve. Always ~–12 LUFS, with organic warmth and acoustic undertones.

7) COLOR / GRADE — Palette: moss green, muted gold, mist gray, dew silver, earth brown. Colors evoke "nostalgic natural softness"—diffuse warm highlights, soft contrast, earthy/sunny feel.

8) NOTES / CONSTRAINTS — 10 seconds, single vertical 9:16 shot, no scene cuts, macro lens and shallow DOF. Mandatory dry whisper at 5.0s: "Moss Chick." Show impossible moss breathing/sprouting. Fade to black with golden particle shimmer. No on-screen text, no extra dialogue.

9) SINGLE-LINE SUMMARY — A 10-second surreal ASMR micro-film for October: Moss Chick — a living baby bird of moss pulses and grows in nostalgic natural softness, captured in tactile macro cinematic detail.

1) STYLE / GENRE — Surreal ASMR micro‑film; haunting, tactile, physically impossible yet plausibly filmed.

2) SCENE DESCRIPTION — In an intimate macro space, a baby chick is composed entirely of rolling, translucent smoke. The bird’s form pulses and reforms: feathers manifest as whorls and filaments, details shimmer and dissipate in continuous, shape-shifting bloom. Every breath and blink causes soft vortex spirals, breaking body borders before reforming. Diffuse, pearly backlight pierces drifting vapor, revealing nested volumes—never cartooned, always smoky and present. No on-screen text.

3) CINEMATOGRAPHY — 9:16 vertical (1080×1920), 80mm macro lens, shallow DOF for severe feather–smoke separation, 180° shutter. Slow dolly-in with gentle left orbit, micro-parallax as smoke layers swirl. Physical inertia kept.

4) ACTIONS & TIMING —
0.0–3.0s: Macro establishing: camera glides in, smoke-chick pulses, hazy threads coil.
3.0–7.0s: Chick shifts and blinks—turbulent smoke feathers bloom, dissolve, and reform impossibly with gesture.
5.0s: Dry, center whisper: “Smoke Chick.”
7.0–10.0s: Macro push to eye; smoky filaments part, shimmer, and drift apart. Slow fade to black through glinting particle mist.

5) AUDIO / ASMR — Vacuum-bed ambience at ~–28 dBFS. Hyper-detailed “smoke” foley: breathy micro-rushes, faint crackling, fluttery pulses. Whisper “Smoke Chick” at 5.0s, dry and centered, no extra lines.

6) MUSIC / SCORE — Ethereal drone score: 0–3s spectral rise, 3–7s swelling airy tones, 7–10s tail off into grainy, dissolving hiss. Maintain ~–12 LUFS.

7) COLOR / GRADE — Palette: opaline white, mist gray, spectral silver, moon blue, faint ash black. Visual grade is cool, matte, and diffused—mirrors the “haunting ephemeral mist” vibe.

8) NOTES / CONSTRAINTS — 10.0s; single continuous shot; vertical 9:16; macro 80mm; shallow DOF; 180° shutter; whisper required at 5.0s; fade to black with shimmering particles; no scene cuts or text; physically impossible form (stable smoke chick) must be the focus.

9) SINGLE-LINE SUMMARY — A 10-second surreal ASMR micro-film for October: Smoke Chick — a baby bird formed from shifting smoke pulses and breathes in haunting, ephemeral macro detail.

1) STYLE / GENRE — Surreal ASMR micro‑film; cinematic, tactile, gentle, physically impossible yet plausibly filmed.

2) SCENE DESCRIPTION — In an intimate macro setting, a newborn chick is entirely formed from luminous, iridescent pearl. Its body shimmers with layers of nacre, radiating shifting colors with every micro-movement. Fine surface ridges ripple with opalescent highlights as the chick breathes, blinks, and gently ruffles its wings; light caustics dance around its outline, and a subtle haze fills the air, catching scattered reflections. No on-screen text.

3) CINEMATOGRAPHY — 9:16 vertical (1080×1920); 80mm lens, shallow DOF focuses closely on the chick’s head and chest. 180° shutter. Slow dolly-in and minor left orbit create parallax between pearlescent shell and warm, diffuse haze. Inertia and macro dynamics observed.

4) ACTIONS & TIMING —
0.0–3.0s: Camera glides in; pearl chick inhales, nacre ripples subtly across its surface.
3.0–7.0s: Chick softly turns, wings flick with luminous, iridescent flashes; surface colors shift, highlights dance.
5.0s: Whisper—dry, center, “Pearl Chick.”
7.0–10.0s: Macro close-up on a single feather as nacre undulates; camera softens, focus drifts as the whole frame fades to black, lingering soft particles shimmer.

5) AUDIO / ASMR — Ambient vacuum-bed at ~–28 dBFS; hyper-detailed foley: delicate shell touches, faint pearl clicks, close mic breaths. Whisper at 5.0s, dry, center: “Pearl Chick.”

6) MUSIC / SCORE — Ethereal, flowing ambient beds with soft bell overtones. 0–3s: lush, gentle chords rise; 3–7s: swelling, crystalline tones; 7–10s: tranquil, luminous tail. Maintain ~–12 LUFS.

7) COLOR / GRADE — Palette: opal white, soft blush pink, cool lavender, misty blue, pale gold. “Serene lustrous calm” expressed via radiant mids, soft contrast, pastel sheen, and diffused ambient light.

8) NOTES / CONSTRAINTS — 10 seconds, single vertical 9:16 shot; 80mm shallow DOF, 180° shutter. No scene cuts or on-screen text. Whisper at 5.0s only (“Pearl Chick”), fade to black with nacreous particle shimmer. Iridescence and shell layering must always be visible, never cartooned.

9) SINGLE-LINE SUMMARY — A 10-second surreal ASMR micro-film for October: Pearl Chick — a luminous, nacre-bodied baby bird breathes and shimmers with serene, opaline calm in impossible tactile detail.

1) STYLE / GENRE — Surreal ASMR micro‑film; tactile, cinematic, sensorial first, physically impossible yet plausibly filmed.

2) SCENE DESCRIPTION — Macro shot on a rough stone surface: a tiny baby chick, but its entire body is transparent, golden honey. The honey forms downy outlines and interior ripples with every subtle movement. Droplets slowly rise from its back, hesitating and floating before defying gravity to rejoin the chick, all in luscious slow motion. Interior glows pulse with each feathery breath. Volumetric warm haze and drifting dust fill the intimate space. No on‑screen text.

3) CINEMATOGRAPHY — 9:16 vertical (1080×1920), 80mm lens, shallow DOF isolating the head and upper body; 180° shutter. Slow dolly-in with gentle leftward orbit, real inertia and macro parallax in the honey and dust motes.

4) ACTIONS & TIMING —
0.0–3.0s: Gentle dolly/orbit establishes the honey chick—surface glow, refraction, and internal ripple seen clearly.
3.0–7.0s: Chick turns, inhales—honey rises in elongated droplets that float then melt back into its wing in impossible, mesmerizing arcs. Camera lingers as pulse glows within the body.
5.0s: Whisper begins, dead center: “Honey Chick.”
7.0–10.0s: Macro close-up as a droplet merges and light ripples inside; slow fade to black as shimmering gold motes drift.

5) AUDIO / ASMR — Deep, soft vacuum-bed at –28 dBFS; tactile, sticky foley: slow honey oozes, faint pops, micro-movements. Whisper “Honey Chick” at 5.0s, dry and centered.

6) MUSIC / SCORE — Ethereal ambient: 0–3s warm ascending pads, 3–7s glowing chords swell gently, 7–10s dissolve to soft, golden tail. Target loudness ~–12 LUFS.

7) COLOR / GRADE — Palette: glowing amber, honey gold, soft chestnut, muted cream, deep bronze. Grading emphasizes “warm viscous glow”: radiant mids and highlights, rich luminous undertones.

8) NOTES / CONSTRAINTS — 10.0 seconds; single continuous vertical shot, 80mm macro; shallow DOF, 180° shutter; 5.0s mandatory dry whisper (“Honey Chick”); descriptor effect (honey, droplets, rising/merging mechanics) always visible and tactile; no on-screen text, only whisper; fade to black with gold particle shimmer.

9) SINGLE-LINE SUMMARY — A 10-second surreal ASMR micro-film for October: Honey Chick — a baby bird formed of pure honey pulses and flows with glowing, impossible warmth in macro cinematic detail.

1) STYLE / GENRE — Surreal ASMR micro‑film; tactile, fluid, cinematic, physically impossible yet viscerally present.

2) SCENE DESCRIPTION — Close-up: a baby bird, rendered entirely from shimmering, translucent liquid, perches atop a matte stone in a softly glowing macro world. Rippling currents run through its body; wings and tiny beak constantly reshape as watery geometry flows, bulges, and reforms. Gravity bends: droplets lift away, float, and spiral gently before threading back into the chick. Ambient mist and suspended particles swirl around in slow eddies. No on-screen text.

3) CINEMATOGRAPHY — 9:16 vertical (1080×1920); 78mm macro lens, shallow DOF isolates the chick’s liquid contours; 180° shutter for gentle motion blur. Slow dolly-in with rightward micro-orbit, parallax in drifting haze and floating droplets. Camera inertia respected.

4) ACTIONS & TIMING —
0.0–3.0s: Camera slides in, revealing the bird’s liquid form: waves glimmer along surface; faint ripples pulse when it blinks.
3.0–7.0s: The chick lifts its watery head; a droplet detaches, dances upward, and spirals in the air before rejoining body—physics feel plausible but impossible.
5.0s: Dry, center whisper: "Liquid Chick."
7.0–10.0s: Ultra-macro focus on a bead unspooling and flowing back; shot softly fades to black as lingering ellipsoidal droplets shimmer in frame.

5) AUDIO / ASMR — Ambience at ~–28 dBFS (soft hush, close); layered with slick ripples, smooth water surface details, miniature flows, drop detach and merge; 5.0s: dry, centered whisper: “Liquid Chick.” No other dialogue.

6) MUSIC / SCORE — Ethereal ambient: watery synths, lush pads, subtle glass resonances. 0–3s rise; 3–7s swell in warmth; 7–10s tail soft and suspended, ~–12 LUFS overall.

7) COLOR / GRADE — Palette: clear water, pale turquoise, soft gold, subtle teal, obsidian black. Grading soft and luminous, with liquid highlights and dreamy, tranquil contrasts—matching “fluid gentle grace” vibe.

8) NOTES / CONSTRAINTS — 10.0 seconds, one continuous vertical shot; no scene cuts. Macro lens, shallow DOF, 180° shutter. Descriptor effect (liquid form with impossible motion) always visible. Whisper at 5.0s: “Liquid Chick” (only), dry, center. Fade to black with signature particle shimmer. No on-screen text.

9) SINGLE-LINE SUMMARY — A 10-second surreal ASMR micro-film for October: Liquid Chick — a baby bird of rippling liquid pulses and recombines in tranquil, impossible detail.

1) STYLE / GENRE — Surreal ASMR micro‑film; tactile, cinematic, physically impossible yet grounded in photographic realism.

2) SCENE DESCRIPTION — In a luminous, close-cropped macro space, a baby chick is sculpted from flawless, transparent crystal. Its facets shimmer, sharp and delicate, refracting beams of light in all directions—edges spray miniature rainbows onto a soft velvet surface. Tiny interior bubbles ride through its form, distorting and twisting the light with every subtle movement. With each blink and breath, cracks spider delicately across its body and heal instantly, visually impossible yet tangible. Ambient dust and suspended particles glow in the diffused light. No on-screen text.

3) CINEMATOGRAPHY — Vertical 9:16 (1080×1920), 80mm lens, shallow DOF isolates the crystal chick’s face and chest; 180° shutter. Smooth dolly-in with a gentle rightward orbit reveals shifting rainbow refractions and dancing highlights, with visible inertia and parallax from floating dust.

4) ACTIONS & TIMING —
0.0–3.0s: Establishing dolly/orbit as crystal chick preens, eye glints sparkle, spectral rays scatter around.
3.0–7.0s: Chick turns; interior bubbles slide; crystal facets pulse with brilliant light; cracks form and vanish in perfect sync with movements. At 5.0s, chick pauses eye-forward.
5.0s: Dry, centered whisper: "Crystal Chick."
7.0–10.0s: Macro close-up on the crystal beak as a prismatic crack heals, color scattering intensifies; dust motes shimmer as shot fades to black with a lingering spectral particle glint.

5) AUDIO / ASMR — Ambient vacuum at ~-28 dBFS, layered with soft crystal chimes, micro glass tinks, delicate foley of rotating mineral facets. 5.0s: dry, dead-center whisper: "Crystal Chick." No additional dialogue.

6) MUSIC / SCORE — Ethereal, crystalline soundscape: thin glass pads, bright harmonics. 0–3s rise with shimmering tones, 3–7s swell of bright, layered intensity, and 7–10s elegant tail, holding ~-12 LUFS.

7) COLOR / GRADE — Palette: ice clear, prism white, radiant silver, spectral blue, flecks of rainbow. Matches "awe transparent intensity"—bright, crisp, dazzling.

8) NOTES / CONSTRAINTS — 10 seconds, single continuous vertical shot (9:16); macro lens; 180° shutter. Mandatory dry whisper "Crystal Chick" at 5.0s, no extra dialogue. Fade to black with signature spectral particle shimmer; no on-screen text. Impossible: instant self-healing cracks, pure prismatic refraction filmed as physical crystal.

9) SINGLE-LINE SUMMARY — A 10-second surreal ASMR micro-film for October: Crystal Chick — a baby bird sculpted from prism-clear crystal pulses with dazzling, impossible light in ultra-tactile cinematic detail.

1) STYLE / GENRE — Surreal ASMR micro‑film; cinematic, tactile, echoic, physically impossible yet plausibly filmed.

2) SCENE DESCRIPTION — A lone, downy chick stands atop an endless, matte obsidian plain inside a quiet, mist-filled studio. The “echo” descriptor manifests as every movement, breath, or peep the chick makes produces luminous, rippling afterimages—soft, translucent echoes of its own form—that radiate and loop back through both air and matte surface. These after-chicks shimmer, multiply, and dissolve in gentle, rhythmic pulses, never quite separating from the original. No on-screen text anywhere.

3) CINEMATOGRAPHY — Vertical 9:16 (1080×1920), 85mm macro lens, shallow DOF isolates the original chick and its afterimage echoes. 180° shutter gives inertia and natural fluidity. Slow dolly-in with subtle rightward orbit, enhancing parallax as echo ripples extend through haze and micro dust.

4) ACTIONS & TIMING —
0.0–3.0s: Establishing macro shot. Camera glides in as subtle echo glows chase the chick’s outline, surface and air shimmer.
3.0–7.0s: Chick blinks, shifts, and peeps; every motion and sound triggers radiating echo duplicates and light ripples, each linger then slowly dissolve back. At 5.0s, the whisper: "Echo Chick."
7.0–10.0s: Extreme close focus on one fading afterimage, light particles swirl, echoes stretch and vanish as the frame fades to black with trailing shimmer.

5) AUDIO / ASMR — Ambient vacuum bed at –28 dBFS; hyper-detailed foley: soft footfalls, faint glassy reverbs, gentle air movement, pulsing duplications. Dry, center-channel whisper at 5.0s: "Echo Chick." No other dialogue.

6) MUSIC / SCORE — Ethereal, glassy drone with subtle harmonic undertones; 0–3s gentle, spectral rise; 3–7s haunting resonance builds in layers; 7–10s long reverberant tail, ~–12 LUFS for all music.

7) COLOR / GRADE — Palette: charcoal black, spectral gray, moonlit silver, mist blue, opaline white. Lighting is cool and reverberant, shadows diffuse, edges vibrate with spectral aura, echoing the “haunting reverberating stillness” vibe.

8) NOTES / CONSTRAINTS — 10.0 seconds, one vertical (9:16) continuous shot, 85mm shallow DOF, 180° shutter; no cuts; single dry whisper at 5.0s must say “Echo Chick”; fade to black with shimmering echo particles; no on-screen text; visible echo/afterimage mechanics are mandatory.

9) SINGLE-LINE SUMMARY — A 10-second surreal ASMR micro-film for October: Echo Chick — a baby bird’s every motion triggers luminous, spectral echoes that ripple through stillness and dissolve in haunting cinematic detail.

1) STYLE / GENRE — Surreal ASMR micro‑film; cinematic, tense, tactile, physically impossible but lens-plausible.

2) SCENE DESCRIPTION — Macro, tightly focused: a baby chick forged entirely from active, rippling shadow perches atop textured, almost black volcanic rock. Instead of feathers, its body is shaped by constantly shifting layers of semi-opaque darkness that flicker and reform—shadow veins flow where down should be. Ambient haze drifts as the shadowed creature blinks; its edges bleed, a hint of translucent light slipping through now and then. Shadows from the chick move counter to the key light, breaking logic. No on-screen text.

3) CINEMATOGRAPHY — Vertical 9:16 (1080×1920), 80mm macro lens, shallow DOF isolates chick’s shifting shadow form. 180° shutter for smooth inertia. Slow, deliberate dolly-in; slight left orbit; parallax from motion in drifting haze and shifting shadows.

4) ACTIONS & TIMING —
0.0–3.0s: Camera glides in, texture of rock and shadowy body revealed, surface flickers tense.
3.0–7.0s: Chick blinks, tiny shadow wings pulse; its shadow splits, then fuses, light briefly leaking through. At 5.0s, camera holds—whisper: “Shadow Chick.”
7.0–10.0s: Macro focus: shadow splits into thin tendrils that swirl, wrap around, then dissolve; slow fade to black with silvery haze and particle shimmer.

5) AUDIO / ASMR — Vacuum-bed ambience at ~–28 dBFS, subtle granular distortion, low-frequency rumbles, micro scratches from rock; deliberate silence between sounds. 5.0s: dry, centered whisper—"Shadow Chick." No other dialogue.

6) MUSIC / SCORE — Ethereal, mysterious dark drone. 0–3s: tension-building low pad; 3–7s: uneasy, pulsing undertones; 7–10s: drawn-out tail into silence; score maintains ~–12 LUFS.

7) COLOR / GRADE — Palette: obsidian black, ash gray, storm blue, highlight silver, bruised violet. Contrast high, details crisp around edges, shadows dense and deep—vibe channels "tense dark mystery".

8) NOTES / CONSTRAINTS — 10.0s, single vertical (9:16) continuous shot; no cuts, no text; whisper at 5.0s (“Shadow Chick” only); shadow body must visibly break light direction/logic; fade to black with shimmering residue.

9) SINGLE-LINE SUMMARY — A 10-second surreal ASMR micro-film for October: Shadow Chick — a baby bird shaped from shifting shadows blinks and pulses against mystery, tension, and impossible living darkness.
